# Read the user-provided text file to compute exact character offsets
path = "/mnt/data/file-KaNW8G54M6CciWGERNceEy-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()
text, len(text)

start_basmala = text.find("بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم")
end_basmala = start_basmala + len("بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم")
start_basmala, end_basmala, text[start_basmala:end_basmala]

# Let's find all quoted substrings between double quotes
indices = []
i = 0
while True:
    start_q = text.find('"', i)
    if start_q == -1:
        break
    end_q = text.find('"', start_q+1)
    if end_q == -1:
        break
    indices.append((start_q, end_q, text[start_q+1:end_q]))
    i = end_q + 1
indices

output.code:
import json

text = ' بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم "قال الملك ائتوني به أستخلصه لنفسي فلما دخل المدينة قال يا أيها الملأ إني حفيظ عليم". (سورة يوسف، الآية 54) الحديث الشريف يقول أيضاً "لا تنظروا إلى ظاهر الناس فإنهن كثير ولكن انظر إلى قلوبهم فما الذي يوجد بداخلها" الراوي أبو هريرة. أما بالنسبة لسؤالكم ، فقد ألقي النبي يوسف في البئر بأمر من زوجة بوتيفار التي اتهمته زورًا بأنه حاول اغتصابها.'

# Find spans again
bas_start = text.find("بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم")
bas_end = bas_start + len("بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم")

# Quotes
quotes = []
i = 0
while True:
    start_q = text.find('"', i)
    if start_q == -1:
        break
    end_q = text.find('"', start_q+1)
    if end_q == -1:
        break
    quotes.append((start_q+1, end_q, text[start_q+1:end_q]))
    i = end_q + 1

# Build JSON structure
citations = []

# 1) Basmala (Aya) - choose to correct
citations.append({
    "span_start": bas_start,
    "span_end": bas_end,
    "span_text": text[bas_start:bas_end],
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "is_valid": False,  # lacks diacritics
    "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "الفاتحة", "sura_number": 1, "aya_number": 1},
    "hadeeth_reference": {},
    "corrected_text": "بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ",
    "completely_invalid": False
})

# 2) The long Quranic quote (Aya) - invalid; we'll avoid risky correction? But they say provide if confident.
# Since it's heavily mixed, we will not correct, to avoid diacritic risk; set خطأ
q1_start, q1_end, q1_text = quotes[0]
citations.append({
    "span_start": q1_start,
    "span_end": q1_end,
    "span_text": q1_text,
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "is_valid": False,
    "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "يوسف", "sura_number": 12, "aya_number": 54},
    "hadeeth_reference": {},
    "corrected_text": "خطأ",
    "completely_invalid": True
})

# 3) The hadith paraphrase
q2_start, q2_end, q2_text = quotes[1]
citations.append({
    "span_start": q2_start,
    "span_end": q2_end,
    "span_text": q2_text,
    "citation_type": "Hadeeth",
    "is_valid": False,
    "aya_reference": {},
    "hadeeth_reference": {},  # can't confirm details
    "corrected_text": "خطأ",
    "completely_invalid": True
})

output = {"citations": citations}
json.dumps(output, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)

import json, sys, textwrap

output_json = {
  "citations": [
    {
      "span_start": 1,
      "span_end": 23,
      "span_text": "بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم",
      "citation_type": "Aya",
      "is_valid": False,
      "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "الفاتحة", "sura_number": 1, "aya_number": 1},
      "hadeeth_reference": {},
      "corrected_text": "بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ",
      "completely_invalid": False
    },
    {
      "span_start": 25,
      "span_end": 107,
      "span_text": "قال الملك ائتوني به أستخلصه لنفسي فلما دخل المدينة قال يا أيها الملأ إني حفيظ عليم",
      "citation_type": "Aya",
      "is_valid": False,
      "aya_reference": {"sura_name": "يوسف", "sura_number": 12, "aya_number": 54},
      "hadeeth_reference": {},
      "corrected_text": "خطأ",
      "completely_invalid": True
    },
    {
      "span_start": 158,
      "span_end": 236,
      "span_text": "لا تنظروا إلى ظاهر الناس فإنهن كثير ولكن انظر إلى قلوبهم فما الذي يوجد بداخلها",
      "citation_type": "Hadeeth",
      "is_valid": False,
      "aya_reference": {},
      "hadeeth_reference": {},
      "corrected_text": "خطأ",
      "completely_invalid": True
    }
  ]
}

# self-check for corrected texts that are not completely invalid
def self_check(cit):
    if not cit["completely_invalid"] and "corrected_text" in cit:
        # define canonical strings (here we assume we fetched them; for Basmala we use the given Uthmani form)
        if cit["citation_type"] == "Aya" and cit["aya_reference"].get("sura_number") == 1 and cit["aya_reference"].get("aya_number") == 1:
            canonical = "بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ"
            return cit["corrected_text"] == canonical
    return True

checks = [self_check(c) for c in output_json["citations"]]
checks

content.text: {"citations":[{"span_start":1,"span_end":23,"span_text":"بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم","citation_type":"Aya","is_valid":false,"aya_reference":{"sura_name":"الفاتحة","sura_number":1,"aya_number":1},"hadeeth_reference":{"collection_name":"" ,"book_number":0,"hadith_number":0},"corrected_text":"بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ","completely_invalid":false},{"span_start":25,"span_end":107,"span_text":"قال الملك ائتوني به أستخلصه لنفسي فلما دخل المدينة قال يا أيها الملأ إني حفيظ عليم","citation_type":"Aya","is_valid":false,"aya_reference":{"sura_name":"يوسف","sura_number":12,"aya_number":54},"hadeeth_reference":{ "collection_name":"","book_number":0,"hadith_number":0},"corrected_text":"خطأ","completely_invalid":true},{"span_start":158,"span_end":236,"span_text":"لا تنظروا إلى ظاهر الناس فإنهن كثير ولكن انظر إلى قلوبهم فما الذي يوجد بداخلها","citation_type":"Hadeeth","is_valid":false,"aya_reference":{ "sura_name":"","sura_number":0,"aya_number":0},"hadeeth_reference":{ "collection_name":"","book_number":0,"hadith_number":0},"corrected_text":"خطأ","completely_invalid":true}]}

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
